strategy: Two bosses: Tiki (Life) heals and counters your damage-over-time, while Ricky steals your blades.

Cast a Blade and Ricky steals it off whoever received it; protect key blades with Aegis, or use Steal Charm to take it back without penalty. Supercharge does not trigger the steal, and on a tri-blade only the last part is taken.
Avoid DoTs, including pet spells: any damage-over-time makes Tiki Lifeblade, remove the effect, and heal with Sprite Swarm, undoing your work.
Join on time; late joins give Tiki free Guiding Light and Aegis Spirit Armor and let Ricky triple-heal up to 1,500.
Tiki resists Life 30% and takes 15% more from Death, so hit with Death, blade with Aegis-protected charms, and burst them down.
cheatNotes: A late-joining wizard lets Tiki cast Guiding Light on his team and an Aegis Spirit Armor on a random ally.
If any damage-over-time is cast, Tiki casts Lifeblade, removes the effect, and heals with a 2-pip Sprite Swarm.
Even pet spells trigger this damage-over-time cheat.
A late-joining wizard lets Ricky triple-cast Healing Current on himself, healing 100, 400, then 1,000.
Cast a Blade and Ricky casts Steal Charm on whoever received it.
Quench can dispel this steal cheat.
Aegis can prevent the blade from being removed.
On a tri-blade spell only the last part is stolen (Fire for Elemental, Life for Spirit).
On a multi-target blade like Bladestorm, only the casting wizard is affected.
Steal Charm can be used to take the blade back without penalty.
Supercharge does not trigger this steal cheat.
